title: pinus laricio var. pyrenaica
intro: Pinus laricio var. pyrenaica, also known as the Pyrenean or Corsican pine, is a variety of evergreen tree native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Pyrenees mountain range. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of terpenes and other bioactive compounds in its resin and needles. The health benefits of Pinus laricio var. pyrenaica include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory conditions, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and anti-infective agent. Herbal preparations of Pinus laricio var. pyrenaica include essential oils, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
